rows.What: Heavy strength set followed by biomechanically similar explosive/plyometric movement
4-exercise complex: heavy compound → plyometric → weighted plyometric/assisted speed work → unweighted speed work, same pattern
rows.Gate: Same gate as above, plus requires adequate plyometric tolerance (no lower-limb injury flags active)

rows.What: Block sequence emphasizing eccentric, then isometric, then concentric focus phases
rows.Gate: `training_age >= 36 months AND goals.primary == "athletic_performance"`; not appropriate for general population hypertrophy/fat-loss goals

rows.Definition: One heaviest working set, followed by sets at reduced load/higher reps
Set broken into mini-sets with short (10-20s) intra-set rest to sustain higher load for more total reps
One activation set to ~RIR1-2, then short-rest mini-sets of 3-5 reps until stimulus fatigue
rows.Gate: Green/yellow tier, isolation exercises preferred over compounds

rows.Definition: Reduce load ~20-30% immediately upon reaching near-failure, continue without rest
rows.Gate: Green tier, isolation/machine exercises preferred (lower injury risk when form degrades)

rows.Definition: Same muscle group, shift to biomechanically easier variation upon failure (e.g., incline → flat → decline press)
Two exercises same muscle group back-to-back, no rest
Two exercises opposing muscle groups back-to-back
rows.Gate: All tiers (efficient, doesn't compound local fatigue)

rows.Definition: Near-failure set, ~15-20s rest, continue same exercise for additional reps

rows.Gate: Green tier only, requires spotter/coach supervision — flag `unsupervised_max_effort_risk` (file 12 Sec 9) if solo

rows.Definition: Slight momentum use to complete reps beyond strict failure
rows.Fatigue Cost: High, elevated injury risk if uncontrolled
rows.Gate: Green tier only, advanced trainees, discouraged for large compound barbell lifts

rows.Definition: Reduced-ROM reps at point of failure to extend set
Restricted venous return with light load (~20-30% 1RM) to induce metabolic stress
rows.Gate: Requires explicit `medical_clearance_required` resolution if any vascular/clotting history flag present (file 12 Sec 4); otherwise green/yellow tier rehab-adjacent use permitted under coach guidance

rows.Definition: Static holds at a joint angle, max or submax effort
rows.Gate: All tiers (yielding isometrics useful even in DEFAULT_SAFE_TEMPLATE progression)

rows.Definition: Submaximal load moved with maximal intended velocity
rows.Gate: Green/yellow tier, requires clean technique baseline first

rows.Edge Case: Client's ideal split (by scoring) conflicts with strong stated preference (e.g., wants bro split, scores favor upper/lower)
rows.Rule: Present scored recommendation with reasoning, but allow client to select preferred split if no safety flag conflict — adherence to a liked plan often outweighs marginal programming optimality; log the override as client-initiated, not system error

rows.Edge Case: Recovery score and goal-fit scores tie between two splits
rows.Rule: Break tie using adherence_fit (Section 1) — favor lower-complexity option for tie-breaking

rows.Edge Case: Client training age unknown/unreliable (file 11 Section 8 discrepancy)
rows.Rule: Default to movement-screen-derived training age, not self-report, per existing file 11 rule

rows.Edge Case: Advanced technique requested by name (e.g., "I want to try myo-reps") by a yellow-tier client
rows.Rule: Explain gate requirement, offer closest permitted alternative (e.g., standard straight sets with slightly higher rep target), do not unlock technique solely on request

rows.Symptom: Split recommendation flip-flops week to week
rows.Cause: Recovery score volatility feeding directly into split selection without smoothing
rows.Fix: Use trailing 2-week average recovery score for split selection, not single latest data point

rows.Symptom: Confidence scores cluster unrealistically high (90%+) across almost all outputs
rows.Cause: `client_data_completeness` and evidence_strength inputs not actually varying / being read
rows.Fix: Audit that Section 5 formula receives real per-recommendation values, not defaulted constants
